backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor defining the backend for a team task manager, including types for Tasks, Users, Roles, and Activity logs, plus CRUD operations for tasks, user registration/login, password reset, team members, and activity logging with access control.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report confirming that the AddUserModal component was updated to remove frontend-side permission guards, leaving only the clean form with Name, Email ID, Role fields, and action buttons.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: A JSON document containing short summaries of all frontend source files, describing the purpose and key details of each component, hook, utility, and page in the React frontend.
frontend/index.css: Global Tailwind CSS entry point defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark theme color tokens including background, foreground, primary, sidebar, and chart colors.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML shell for the React app, mounting the root div and loading the main TypeScript entry point as an ES module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that sets up TanStack Router with routes for Dashboard, Tasks, Activity Log, Team Members, and My Tasks pages, wrapped in QueryClientProvider and AuthProvider with no authentication-gated routes.
frontend/src/components/AddUserModal.tsx: Modal dialog component for adding a new team member with Name, Email, and Role fields; performs client-side validation and calls the useAddTeamMember mutation, with no permission guard or error display for unauthorized users.
Modal dialog form for creating a new task with fields for title, conference name, description, assignee, deadline, and priority; converts the deadline to nanoseconds and submits via the useCreateTask mutation.
frontend/src/components/EditTaskModal.ts: A modal dialog component for editing an existing task, pre-populating fields (title, description, assignee, deadline, priority, conference name) from the task prop and submitting updates via the `useEditTask` mutation hook.
frontend/src/components/GoogleSignInButt: Empty file with no content or exports.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Main application layout component providing a responsive sidebar with navigation links, a theme switcher, notification bell, and a static admin user card, wrapping page content passed as children.
Empty file with no content or exports.
frontend/src/components/NotificationBell: A notification bell icon component that displays a dropdown panel showing overdue/upcoming task reminders and recent login events (admin-only), with a badge count for total unread notifications.
frontend/src/components/PriorityBadge.ts: A small presentational component that renders a colored, emoji-labeled badge for a task's priority level (High, Medium, or Low) with configurable size.
frontend/src/components/ReportExportButt: Provides export buttons for downloading task data as CSV files filtered by the current week, current month, or a user-defined custom date range via a popover.
frontend/src/components/TaskCard.tsx: Displays a single task's details (title, priority, urgency, assignee, deadline, status) in a styled card with collapsible description, inline status control, and optional edit/delete actions.
frontend/src/components/TaskFilters.tsx: A filter bar component offering a text search input and an assignee dropdown to filter the task list, with a clear button that appears when any filter is active.
frontend/src/components/TaskStatusContro: A dropdown control component that allows updating a task's status, showing a loading spinner during the async update and displaying success/error toasts.
frontend/src/components/TeamMemberCard.t: An expandable card component displaying a team member's task summary (counts by status) with a collapsible list of their sorted tasks.
frontend/src/components/ThemeSwitcher.ts: A button group component that lets users switch between light, normal, and dark themes, persisting the selection via the useTheme hook.
frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx: Provides a hardcoded always-authenticated admin AuthContext with no-op login/logout stubs, making the app permanently signed in as an admin user.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: A hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor using the current Internet Identity, initializing access control with an admin token and invalidating dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts: A simple re-export of the useAuth hook from AuthContext.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Implements an Internet Identity authentication provider and hook using DFINITY's AuthClient, managing login status, identity persistence, and delegation validity.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Centralizes all React Query hooks for backend interactions including tasks, users, team members, and activity entries, wrapping actor calls with proper cache invalidation.
frontend/src/hooks/useTaskFilters.ts: A memoized hook that filters a list of tasks by search text and assignee name based on provided filter state.
frontend/src/hooks/useTaskReminders.ts: A hook that categorizes all tasks into reminder buckets (overdue, due in 1 hour, due in 24 hours, carry forward) and returns counts for notification purposes.
frontend/src/hooks/useTheme.ts: A hook that manages the app's theme (dark/light/normal) by applying CSS classes to the document root and persisting the selection to localStorage.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that imports Google Fonts and defines Tailwind base/components/utilities, plus CSS custom properties for dark, light, and normal themes covering colors, sidebar, avatars, and urgency levels.
frontend/src/main.tsx: React app entry point that mounts the app with QueryClientProvider, InternetIdentityProvider, and the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/ActivityLog.tsx: Page that fetches and displays all activity log entries with filtering by actor, task title, and date range, rendered as a sorted timeline with colored action badges.
frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.tsx: Admin dashboard page showing task/member stats, a tabbed view of all tasks and team members, and modals for creating, editing, and deleting tasks and adding users.
frontend/src/pages/Tasks.tsx: Tasks page that lists all tasks with search/assignee filtering, sorting by deadline and priority, and admin controls for creating, editing, and deleting tasks.
frontend/src/pages/TeamMembers.tsx: React page that displays team members list with role badges, allows admins to add or remove members via modal and mutation hooks, and shows loading/empty states.
frontend/src/pages/TeamMemberView.tsx: Page that displays tasks assigned to a selected team member with stats cards for pending, in-progress, completed, and overdue counts.
frontend/src/utils/csvExport.ts: Utility functions for generating, downloading, and filtering task data as CSV, including helpers for current week, current month, and custom date range filtering.
frontend/src/utils/icErrors.ts: Parses Internet Computer rejection errors (by reject code or message patterns) into user-friendly error strings for display in the UI.
frontend/src/utils/reminderLogic.ts: Provides helper functions to check if a task is overdue, due within one hour, or due within twenty-four hours based on its nanosecond deadline.
frontend/src/utils/taskSorting.ts: Exports a single function that sorts an array of tasks by deadline ascending, with priority as a tiebreaker.
frontend/src/utils/taskUrgency.ts: Determines a task's urgency level (overdue, critical, warning, normal, completed) and provides corresponding CSS class strings and display labels for UI rendering.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routes, persisting them in sessionStorage, and cleaning sensitive params from the URL bar.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration that maps OKLCH CSS variables to semantic color tokens, extends border radius and box shadow, sets custom fonts, and registers animation and typography plugins.
project_state.json: Tracks project state for fixing the 'Add Team Member' modal, including removing frontend permission guards and backend role-based restrictions so any authenticated user can add team members.
